name: System Component Filters
description: Filter graph nodes by system component and architectural layer

filters.name: Core AI Systems
filters.description: Show AI engines, agents, and cognitive systems
filters.useCase: Focus on AI/ML components and intelligent systems

filters.name: Security Systems
filters.description: Show security modules, authentication, and encryption systems

filters.name: Governance Framework
filters.description: Show governance policies and constitutional AI frameworks
filters.useCase: Review ethical guidelines and compliance requirements

filters.name: GUI Components
filters.description: Show PyQt6 UI components and desktop interfaces

filters.name: Data & Persistence
filters.description: Show data systems, databases, and persistence layers

filters.name: Web Services
filters.description: Show web services, REST APIs, and frontend components

filters.name: Integration Points
filters.description: Show external integrations and event systems
filters.useCase: Map system boundaries and external dependencies

filters.name: Infrastructure & DevOps
filters.description: Show infrastructure, deployment, and CI/CD systems

filters.name: Testing Framework
filters.description: Show test suites and QA frameworks
filters.useCase: Test coverage analysis and QA strategy

business-logic.description: Core application logic and business rules

applyingFilters: Select system components to focus on specific subsystems
Combine with architectural layers for structured views
Use dependency mapping to trace system interactions

systemViews.frontendDevelopment: Show: gui-layer, web-layer | Hide: infrastructure, testing
systemViews.backendDevelopment: Show: core-ai, data-layer, integration-layer | Hide: gui, web
systemViews.architectureReview: Show: All layers | Use color schemes to differentiate

dependencyAnalysis.upstreamImpact: Select component, show all dependencies it requires
dependencyAnalysis.downstreamImpact: Select component, show all systems depending on it
dependencyAnalysis.isolationAnalysis: Find components with minimal dependencies (good for microservices)
